column8: Many of us get our news and entertainment from television and the Internet. You might be surprised to learn, then, that people who listen to the radio are much happier than___________people who use the other media outlets. (THAT)
column9: It makes perfect sence when you think about it. Television and the Internet offer entertainment, but negative activity_______________in the mix as well. We sometimes see saddening news reports, and we do tedious things online, such as check account balances. (INCLUDE)
column10: With radio, people listen to it because they want to feel good. It seems that in recent years we__________about the joy radio gives us because we focus on television and the Internet so much. (FORGET)
column11: Leslie was under a lot of pressure at work. Her boss wasn't certain that she would get everything done, but she reassured him by saying, "I promise you, the project____________on Friday." (COMPLETE)
column12: The truth was it would take a miracle to finish the work by then. She thought about asking her boss for more time, but as she____________it on Friday she felt it was too late to ask now. (PROMISE)
column13: On Thursday, when Leslie was stressed the most, she_____________her ankle while she was climbing some stairs and she had to go to hospital immediately. (BREAK)
column14: She thought it might be the "break" she needed. But while she was happy to get out of work, she___________forward to wearing a cast o her leg for two months. (NOT LOOK)
